findings.issue: Tasks 2, 3, 4, 6, 7 have descriptions that run 4-6 sentences — longer than the 2-3 sentence guidance. They still stay scope-level (no step-by-step, no line numbers, no AC bullets), but verge on implementation detail (e.g. Task 4 describes SAF launcher callbacks step by step).
findings.fix: Optionally compress Task 2/3/4/6/7 descriptions to 2-3 sentences and push behavioural detail into the task files produced during decomposition. Not blocking — content is scope-appropriate and references Decisions by number rather than re-explaining rationale.

findings.issue: Task 10 uses skill `test-master` which is in the catalog, but the Skill → Reviewers Mapping lists `test-master` as 'auditor IS the review'. The task correctly has `Reviewers: none`. No issue — logged only as confirmation of an easy-to-miss catalog match (older drafts sometimes used `test-auditor`).

summary: Tech-spec is template-compliant and ready for decomposition. Frontmatter (created/status=draft/branch=dev/size=M) is correct. All required sections are present and non-empty, including Shared Resources and User-Spec Deviations (explicitly 'None' with justification). Decisions 1-11 each have Decision/Rationale/Alternatives; Decision 5 is correctly marked [TECHNICAL], the rest anchor to user-spec. Implementation Tasks count 11 (within limit of 15). Skills and reviewers all map to valid catalog entries (`code-writing`→code-reviewer/security-auditor/test-reviewer; `code-reviewing`/`security-auditor`/`test-master` in Audit Wave with no reviewers; `pre-deploy-qa` in Final Wave). Audit Wave contains all three required audits (Code/Security/Test). Final Wave has Pre-deploy QA; Deploy and Post-deploy are explicitly omitted with HTML-comment justification citing the mobile/no-CI nature of the project, which matches the stated policy. Wave conflict analysis: Wave 1 tasks touch disjoint files (strings.xml, crash/*, logexport/*); Wave 2 tasks touch disjoint files (CrashReportActivity+RestartCrashBanner, AndroidManifest+SanctumApplication, AboutScreen, ModelManagerScreen) — no parallel-wave conflicts. No AC bullets, TDD anchors, or line-number references inside any task. Technical rationale stays in the Decisions section; tasks reference decisions by number instead of re-explaining. Two minor observations recorded (description length in a few tasks, catalog cross-check) — neither blocks approval.
